food_subgroup: Fast food combo meal — burger, fried chicken burger, fries, and carbonated beverage

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for McDonald's Singapore combo meal. Mapped to closest parent class 'fast food meal (food product)' FOODON_00002383.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Fast foods) used as the closest defensible category anchor. No specific code exists for this branded McDonald's Singapore combo meal.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No applicable SNOMED CT food concept identified for this branded fast food combo meal.

serving.description: 1 combo meal (Creamy Mushroom Deluxe burger + McSpicy burger + 2x medium fries + 2x small Coke)
serving.natural_language_portion: One full combo meal consisting of one Creamy Mushroom Deluxe burger, one McSpicy fried chicken burger, two medium portions of French fries, and two small Coca-Cola drinks, totalling approximately 1050 g.

nutrient_source.title: McDonald's Singapore Nutrition Information — Creamy Mushroom Deluxe, McSpicy, Medium French Fries, Small Coca-Cola
nutrient_source.organization: McDonald's Singapore / USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy) used for component cross-validation
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are estimated by summing published McDonald's Singapore per-item nutrition data for: Creamy Mushroom Deluxe burger (~220 g, ~490 kcal), McSpicy burger (~200 g, ~490 kcal), 2x Medium Fries (~2×114 g, ~2×320 kcal), 2x Small Coke (~2×250 ml, ~2×90 kcal). Total meal ~1050 g, ~1800 kcal. Per-100g values derived by dividing total meal nutrients by 10.5. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy used for secondary cross-validation of macro ratios. This is a composite meal estimate; individual item values may vary by outlet.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database analogous food references for white bread bun, French fries, and cola beverage
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific McDonald's Singapore combo meal. GI estimated as a weighted composite using University of Sydney GI Database values for analogous components: white bread burger bun GI ~71 (Atkinson et al. 2008), McDonald's French fries GI ~63 (University of Sydney GI Database, tested), cola beverage GI ~63 (University of Sydney GI Database). Weighted composite GI ~65 (medium). Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 222.6) / 100 ≈ 145. Carbohydrate per serving estimated at ~222.6 g for the full 1050 g meal.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content (~3098 mg per meal serving)
hypertension.guidance: This combo meal provides approximately 3098 mg of sodium per serving, exceeding the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg in a single meal; individuals with hypertension should avoid this meal or request reduced-sodium modifications and limit other sodium sources throughout the day.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (~145 per meal) and high refined carbohydrate content (~222 g per meal)
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a glycemic load of approximately 145 per serving and over 220 g of rapidly digestible carbohydrates from buns, fries, and cola, this meal is likely to cause a significant postprandial blood glucose spike and is not suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes without substantial portion reduction and substitution of the cola with water.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~26 g per meal), trans fat (~1 g per meal), and sodium (~3098 mg per meal)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of high saturated fat, trans fat, and sodium in this meal poses a significant cardiovascular risk; individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should limit consumption and consider replacing fries with a salad and the cola with water to reduce atherogenic and hypertensive load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Creamy Mushroom Deluxe and McSpicy Meal Combo is a McDonald's Singapore promotional combo meal consisting of two signature burgers — the Creamy Mushroom Deluxe (a beef burger topped with a rich mushroom cream sauce, lettuce, tomato, and cheese on a sesame bun) and the McSpicy (a deep-fried spicy seasoned chicken thigh fillet burger) — served alongside two medium portions of McDonald's French fries and two small Coca-Cola drinks. The meal is a high-calorie, high-sodium, ultra-processed fast food offering designed for sharing or as a value bundle. The Creamy Mushroom Deluxe delivers a savoury, umami-rich flavour profile from the mushroom cream sauce, while the McSpicy provides a crispy, spicy heat from its seasoned breading. The fries are salted and crispy, and the cola adds sweetness and carbonation. Nutritionally, the full meal provides approximately 1800 kcal, 61 g protein, 80 g fat (including ~26 g saturated fat), and ~222 g carbohydrates, with a very high sodium load of ~3098 mg. The glycemic load is very high (~145), making this meal clinically significant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The meal is certified Halal by McDonald's Singapore and is available at McDonald's outlets across Singapore. It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or individuals on low-sodium or low-glycaemic diets without significant modification.

How many calories in McDonald's Creamy Mushroom Deluxe and McSpicy combo?

Creamy Mushroom Deluxe burger calories Singapore
Is the Creamy Mushroom McSpicy meal suitable for diabetics?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are estimated from published McDonald's Singapore per-item nutrition data summed across combo components and converted to per-100g basis. No single approved primary source (HPB Foodo, USDA) contains an exact entry for this specific combo meal. McDonald's Singapore nutrition page is used as the closest available branded source; USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy used for cross-validation. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database component values. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. This is a composite combo meal record. Per-100g values are derived by dividing estimated total meal nutrient content by the estimated total meal weight (1050 g). Individual outlet preparation may vary. 2. Vitamin A (mcg RAE) is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. Vitamin B1 (thiamine mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. Vitamin B2 (riboflavin mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. Vitamin B3 (niacin mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. Vitamin B6 (mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. Vitamin B12 (mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. Folate (mcg DFE) is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. Vitamin D (mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. Vitamin E (mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. Phosphorus (mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. Magnesium (mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. Zinc (mg) is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. Selenium (mcg) is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. These micronutrients are not individually reported in McDonald's Singapore's published nutrition summary for this combo, and no approved primary source provides complete micronutrient data for this specific meal combination. 3. The food_group has been corrected from the input value 'Vegetables' to 'Mixed', as this is a composite fast food combo meal containing burgers, fries, and beverages. 4. GI is an analogous composite estimate; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific meal. 5. Sodium per serving is very high (~3098 mg), exceeding WHO daily recommended intake in a single meal. 6. The meal is listed as Halal by McDonald's Singapore. 7. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol, vitamin C, and water values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component analogues and McDonald's Singapore published data.
